name: 马善高
current_positions: Minister for Social and Family Development
Vice-Chairman of the People's Action Party
Member of Parliament (Tampines West, Tampines GRC)

education.field: Electrical and Electronic Engineering (First Class Honours)

education.institution: University of Southern California (USC Marshall School of Business)

work_experience.position: Electrical engineer; rose to CEO of SingTel Global Offices
Member of Parliament (Tampines West, Tampines GRC)

Ministry of Home Affairs / Ministry of Foreign Affairs
Ministry of the Environment and Water Resources
Ministry of Social and Family Development

person_relationships.description: PAP team member together in Tampines GRC for GE2025; Masagos introduced Neo to Tampines constituents
Succeeded Yaacob Ibrahim as Minister-in-Charge of Muslim Affairs in 2018
Faishal Ibrahim succeeded Masagos as Acting Minister-in-Charge of Muslim Affairs in May 2025
Succeeded Desmond Lee as Minister for Social and Family Development in 2020

political_stances.stance_content: Said his elevation to full Minister reflected 'the progress of the Malay community' in Singapore; first time two Malay full ministers served in Cabinet simultaneously

Opposed allowing tudung (hijab) as part of nurse's uniform, arguing it would 'raise a very visible religious marker' with significant implications for patient preferences; urged religious discussions be done 'behind closed doors'

Emphasized sustaining social mobility across generations and building inclusive society; implemented Singapore Made for Families initiatives to support all families to be stable, self-reliant, and attain social mobility
At UN World Summit for Social Development 2025, highlighted Singapore's focus on families and communities in tackling poverty and promoting social inclusion

major_achievements.achievement: Promoted to full Minister; first time in Singaporean history with two Malay ministers in the Cabinet (alongside Yaacob Ibrahim)
Appointed Minister-in-Charge of Muslim Affairs; helmed formation of M3 initiative to synergise efforts to build the Malay-Muslim community
Led COVID-19 response through national support schemes for families in need; developed 48 Family Service Centres network
Implemented Singapore Made for Families initiatives supporting families to be stable, self-reliant, and attain social mobility
Appointed Vice-Chairman of the People's Action Party
Led PAP team in Tampines GRC to narrow victory with 52.02% against Workers' Party (47.37%) in a four-cornered fight

biography_summary: Masagos Zulkifli bin Masagos Mohamad (born 16 April 1963) is a Singaporean politician who has served as Vice-Chairman of the PAP since 2025 and Minister for Social and Family Development since 2020. Before politics, he was an electrical engineer at Singtel, rising to CEO of SingTel Global Offices. He holds a BEng (First Class Honours) from NTU, MSc from NUS, and MBA from USC. He entered politics in 2006 as MP for Tampines GRC and has held various portfolios: Education, Home Affairs, Foreign Affairs, Environment and Water Resources (2015-2020), and Social and Family Development (2020-present). He was the first Malay full Minister appointed alongside another Malay minister (Yaacob Ibrahim) in 2015. He served as Minister-in-Charge of Muslim Affairs (2018-2025) and helmed the M3 initiative for the Malay-Muslim community.
